assessment_objectives.objective: the type of agreement used to approve and manage the exchange of information is defined (e.g., interconnection security agreements, information exchange security agreements, memoranda of understanding or agreement, service level agreements, user agreements, non-disclosure agreements or organization-defined type of agreements).
security requirements for each system are documented as part of the exchange agreements.
controls are documented as part of each exchange agreement.
the frequency at which to review and update agreements is defined.
the exchange of sensitive / regulated data between the system and other systems is approved using organization-defined values.
the exchange of sensitive / regulated data between the system and other systems is managed using organization-defined values.
interface characteristics for each system are documented as part of the exchange agreements.
responsibilities for each system are documented as part of the exchange agreements.
the impact level of the information communicated is documented as part of each exchange agreement.
exchange agreements are reviewed per an organization-defined frequency.
exchange agreements are updated per an organization-defined frequency.
systems are prohibited from directly connecting to an external network is defined.
the boundary protection device required for a direct connection to an external network is defined.
the direct connection of systems to an external network without the use of boundary protection device is prohibited.
the direct connection of classified national security system to an external network without the use of an organization-defined boundary protection device is prohibited.
approved authorizations are enforced for controlling the flow of CUI between connected systems.
